statement: Customer feedback is scattered across Slack and easily missed
quote: we have a Slack channel where the support team drops customer complaints. I try to read it every morning but honestly I miss half of them

statement: Product decisions are based on memory rather than data
quote: Mostly my memory. I remember the loudest complaints

statement: Built a feature based on one loud customer that nobody else wanted
quote: we built a dashboard feature because one enterprise client kept asking for it. Turned out nobody else wanted it. We wasted three weeks of engineering time

statement: Engineering team lost trust in product decisions and demands evidence
quote: He said show me the data, show me the customer quotes, stop coming to me with gut feelings

statement: Sprint planning feels adversarial due to lack of evidence
quote: every sprint planning feels like a courtroom. I am the lawyer trying to convince the jury. But I have no evidence

statement: Nobody watches full call recordings to find relevant quotes
quote: nobody watches a 45 minute recording

statement: Existing transcription tools produce unstructured walls of text
quote: We tried Otter for transcription but it just gives you a wall of text. Finding the one quote I need takes longer than the original call

statement: Wants to upload a call and get pain points with exact quotes attached
quote: I want to upload a Zoom call and get back a list of pain points with the exact customer quote attached

statement: Wants clickable links to the exact moment a customer described a problem
quote: I can click a link and play the exact 30 seconds where the customer described the problem

statement: Wants actual verbatim quotes not AI summaries
quote: Not a summary. The actual words they said

statement: Quarterly feedback synthesis takes two full weeks of manual work
quote: Last quarter it took me two full weeks just to go through all the notes and group them into themes

statement: Not confident all insights are captured during manual synthesis
quote: I was not confident I caught everything. Some insights definitely fell through the cracks

statement: Missed a pattern of confusing API error messages that multiple customers reported independently
quote: three different customers independently told us the error messages were confusing. But I only found out when a support ticket escalated

statement: Research tools are fragmented and do not integrate with each other
quote: none of them talk to each other. I end up copying and pasting quotes between three different tools. It is a mess

statement: Wants automated pattern detection across all call recordings
quote: I want to dump all my call recordings into one place and have it tell me the top five things customers are struggling with

statement: Wants real quotes not AI-generated summaries that hallucinate
quote: With the actual quotes. Not AI summaries that make things up

statement: Has been burned by ChatGPT hallucinating customer quotes
quote: I have been burned by ChatGPT hallucinating customer quotes before

statement: Team adoption is low despite six months of paying
quote: we have been paying twelve hundred dollars a month for six months and I still cannot get my team to use it consistently

statement: Onboarding was confusing for the team
quote: The onboarding was confusing, the UI keeps changing, and nobody on my team trusts the data it shows

statement: Analytics dashboard showed wildly inaccurate feature adoption numbers
quote: the analytics dashboard showed us that feature adoption was at eighty percent. But when we manually checked, it was closer to thirty

statement: Wrong data ended up in a board presentation causing embarrassment
quote: My VP saw the wrong number in a board deck. That was embarrassing

statement: Churn prediction model completely failed to identify at-risk accounts
quote: the churn prediction model flagged zero risk accounts for three months straight while we were actually losing customers

statement: Support response times are unacceptably slow
quote: The first ticket took eleven days to get a response

statement: Support replies are generic and do not address the actual problem
quote: The second time we got a canned reply that did not address the problem

statement: Known bug was promised to be fixed but remains unfixed after four months
quote: said it was a known issue that would be fixed in the next release. That was four months ago. Still not fixed

statement: Salesforce integration works seamlessly
quote: The integration with Salesforce was actually great. That part was seamless

statement: Weekly email digest is simple, clear and useful
quote: the weekly email digest that summarizes account health, my team loved that. Simple, clear, useful

statement: Pricing is too expensive for a product where half the features are unreliable
quote: Twelve hundred a month for a team of five is steep when half the features do not work reliably

statement: Sales rep had no flexibility and felt impersonal during renewal negotiation
quote: She kept reading from a script. That was the final straw. I felt like just another number

statement: Wants per-seat pricing based on active users instead of rigid tier bundles
quote: let us pay per seat. We have five users but only three are active. Why am I paying for seats nobody uses

statement: No onboarding guidance after signup — dropped into dashboard with no tutorial
quote: I was dropped into the dashboard with no guidance. There was no tutorial, no walkthrough, nothing

statement: Documentation is written for developers not product managers
quote: the docs but they are clearly written for developers. I am a product manager. When I see instructions like run this curl command in your terminal I close the tab

statement: Needs a simple drag and drop interface for uploading data
quote: I need a simple drag and drop interface. Just let me upload a CSV and show me results

statement: File upload failed silently with no error message or progress indicator
quote: the upload failed silently. No error message, no progress bar, nothing. The screen just sat there

statement: Schema validation error did not specify which column was wrong
quote: it gave me an error that said invalid schema but did not tell me which column was wrong. I had forty columns

statement: Support agent Kavita gave an excellent and fast response
quote: Kavita replied within two hours with a screenshot showing exactly which columns needed to be renamed. She was fantastic

statement: Should not need to email support just to upload a file
quote: I should not need to email support just to upload a file

statement: Trend charts are beautiful and impressed the CEO
quote: The trend charts are beautiful. I actually showed them to my CEO and she was impressed

statement: Needs chart export to PNG or PDF for board decks
quote: I cannot export them. I need to put these charts into my monthly board deck and there is no export to PNG or PDF option

statement: No role-based access control — invited users can see billing and API keys
quote: I invited my analyst to the workspace but she can see everything including billing information and API keys. There is no role-based access

statement: Needs viewer role that hides sensitive settings
quote: I need to be able to invite someone as a viewer without exposing sensitive settings

statement: Search functionality is the best among any SaaS tool used
quote: The search functionality is the best I have used in any SaaS tool. I can find any customer conversation in seconds

statement: User actively refers the product to friends and colleagues
quote: I have actually told three other PM friends about it and two of them signed up

statement: Mobile app feels like an afterthought with tiny text and hard-to-tap buttons
quote: the mobile experience feels like an afterthought. Text is tiny, buttons are hard to tap, and the app crashes at least once a day

statement: Mobile app crashes daily and bug reports are ignored
quote: the app crashes at least once a day. I have reported the crash three times and keep getting told it is on the roadmap

statement: Notification system is overwhelming with no way to customize preferences
quote: I get notified about everything. Every comment, every tag, every status change. There is no way to customize notification preferences

statement: Wants granular notification preferences to choose what to be notified about
quote: Just let me choose what I want to be notified about

statement: Real-time collaboration on documents has replaced Google Docs entirely
quote: The real-time collaboration on documents is incredible. We have completely replaced Google Docs for our product specs

statement: Linking specs to customer evidence is a killer feature no other tool offers
quote: the fact that I can link specs to customer evidence is the killer feature. No other tool does that

statement: Needs Jira integration so insights automatically sync as linked evidence
quote: I need a Jira integration. Right now I manually copy insight summaries into Jira tickets

statement: Wants an API to build custom dashboards combining product data with internal metrics
quote: I want an API so I can build custom dashboards. Your built-in dashboards are fine but I need to combine your data with our internal metrics

statement: Pricing page feature comparison is unclear when trying to upgrade
quote: Your pricing page is confusing. I wanted to upgrade my team from the growth plan to the enterprise plan but the feature comparison table does not clearly explain what we would get

statement: Slow sales response caused a lost twelve thousand dollar upgrade deal
quote: I emailed sales and they took five days to reply. By then my budget approval window had closed. You literally lost a twelve thousand dollar deal because your sales team was slow
